title: epilobium tetragonum subsp. virgatum
intro: Epilobium tetragonum subsp. virgatum, also known as the triangular-fruited willowherb, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Onagraceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are believed to contribute to its potential health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and protecting against cell damage. The health benefits of Epilobium tetragonum subsp. virgatum are thought to be attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can be extracted and used in herbal preparations. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made from the leaves and stems, and are often used in tea or capsule form to support overall health and well-being.
